BOISE, July 20.-Taking up the
attempts to kill Peabody and Cab
bart, as testified by Orchard, Haw-
ley today went deeply into the
question of motive.


who would want these men out
of the way, who would benefit II
they were killed. tell you it
would be the Western Federation
Of Miners and no one else. They
plotted these crimes. This man
Haywood advocated and endorsed
them In every way. and now he
would evade the responsibility by
endeavoring to cause a doubt of
Orchards narrative.


when you look at the entire
matter you will know at once how
and why the bombs were planted.
1t was to terrorize the state admin
istration and judiciary and compel
them to work with these so-called
labor leaders"


Hawley was still talking when a
recess was ordered.


The defense suffered a heavy re-
verse yesterday afternoon when
Judge Wood decided to exclude all
evidence duplicating the, Mine
Owners association and the Pin
kertons in a counter conspiracy
against the miners union. This
bars from consideration by the
jury-all the testimony as to the de-
portation of miners from Colorado,
their prosecutions and the illegal
acts alleged against the mine own
ers In efforts to destroy the union.
The decision supports the prosecu-
tion's evidence brought to substan-
tiate Orchards story, and denies
the defense testimony to Offset this
on the ground that the defense fail.
ed to establish corroborative evi.
dence of conspiracy against the
miners.
